Source: Cursor Documentation
URL: https://docs.cursor.com (redirects to cursor.com/docs)
Fetched: 2026-03-22

# Cursor Documentation

## Core Description
Cursor is an AI editor and coding agent designed to help developers understand codebases, plan and build features, fix bugs, review changes, and integrate with existing tools.

## Key Features
- AI editor and coding agent
- Code understanding, feature development, bug fixing, code review
- Rapid prototyping
- Test generation, documentation writing, legacy code modernization

## Start Here
- Get started: Go from install to your first useful change in Cursor
- Models & Pricing: Compare models, usage pools, and plan pricing
- Changelog: Links to recent updates and feature releases

## Core Capabilities
- Understanding codebases
- Planning and building features
- Bug fixing and debugging
- Code review capabilities
- Integration with development tools
- Support for multiple programming languages (Python, JavaScript/TypeScript, Java, C#, iOS/macOS/Swift, data science workflows, web development)
- Cloud agent capabilities
- API access and automation
- Team collaboration features

## Documentation Structure
The site includes comprehensive guides for:
- Configuration and setup
- CLI tools and commands
- Cloud agent deployment
- Enterprise features
- Team management and billing
- Model selection and pricing
- Security and compliance
- Integration with external services (GitHub, GitLab, Linear, Slack, etc.)

## Security & Enterprise
- SSO and SCIM provisioning
- Admin APIs for organizational management
- Compliance monitoring and audit trails
- Network configuration for enterprise
- Service accounts for automated workflows

## Extensions & Integrations
- GitHub, GitLab, Linear, Slack
- JetBrains IDE support
- VS Code migrations
- Cloud Agent with automations, security monitoring, private workers
